simple_verdict: Critical alert: We have plenty of site visitors, but a major issue is stopping them from reaching the goal page and completing their orders.
what_happened: Site traffic was actually 20% higher than our benchmark, but goal page visits plummeted by 90%. Because so few people reached the goal page, the number of created orders dropped by 20%. Finally, the success rate for those orders also fell, resulting in a 38% drop in successful orders compared to the benchmark.
main_insight: Traffic was okay, but conversion got weaker. The primary failure is happening right at the beginning of the journey, as visitors are failing to move from the landing page to the goal page. A secondary issue is causing a higher-than-normal failure rate at the final checkout step.
likely_reasons: A broken link, missing button, or page crash is preventing visitors from navigating to the goal page.
A tracking glitch is causing the system to miss counting the goal page visits.
A payment gateway issue is causing customers who try to check out to fail at the final step.
what_to_check_now: Manually test the website journey from the homepage to the goal page to see if it is broken.
Ask the technical team to verify if analytics tracking for the goal page was recently changed or broken.
Review payment processing logs to find out why created orders are not turning into successful orders.
one_line_summary: Despite healthy traffic, a massive 90% drop in goal page visits and a struggling checkout process are severely reducing successful orders.
